title: Deterministic CrowdAnki JSON → .apkg build tool
context: Anki decks are distributed as .apkg files, but the source of truth for open-flashcards is CrowdAnki JSON stored in version control. A reproducible build tool is needed to convert JSON → .apkg deterministically so that the same source always produces the same binary, enabling verification, CI, and non-destructive updates. This is the core infrastructure that unblocks all deck publishing.
objective: Build a CLI tool that converts CrowdAnki-aligned JSON (per the data-001 schema) to a valid .apkg file deterministically, with no baked-in scheduling state.
acceptanceCriteria: Reads CrowdAnki JSON from the decks/ directory and produces a valid .apkg file using genanki or equivalent.
Build is deterministic: identical input JSON always produces an identical .apkg (same byte hash).
Preserves GUIDs exactly as specified in the JSON; does not generate new GUIDs or modify existing ones.
Strips any scheduling state (due dates, review history) from the output .apkg so it is scheduler-agnostic.
Validates the input JSON against the data-001 schema before building; exits non-zero with a clear error if validation fails.
Includes a README explaining usage and a minimal test suite; licensed MIT.
output: A merged PR adding a build tool (tools/build-apkg/) that converts CrowdAnki JSON to .apkg, with tests and README, licensed MIT.
